title: Blysmus compressus
intro: Blysmus compressus, also known as flat-stemmed woodrush or woodrush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Juncaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Blysmus compressus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, and it has also been used as a natural remedy for fever and sore throats.
taxonomy: Blysmus compressus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is commonly known as flat-stemmed blysmus. It is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The classification of Blysmus compressus is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the class Liliopsida. Blysmus compressus is a monocotyledonous plant, meaning it has one cotyledon in its seeds.
uses: Blysmus compressus, also known as flat-stemmed woodrush or flat woodrush, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and rhizomes are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. Additionally, Blysmus compressus is thought to have diuretic properties, making it useful in treating urinary tract issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Blysmus compressus for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Blysmus compressus is also known as round-leaved bog sedge, however, information about its side effects is limited.
